target.title: fix(codex): honor workspace-only fs for native tools
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
When an OpenClaw agent sets `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true`, OpenClaw-owned file tools enforce a workspace boundary. Codex app-server code mode also has a native shell/filesystem surface. Current Codex `workspace-write` is not a true OpenClaw workspace-only read boundary, so OpenClaw must fail closed for that native surface instead of projecting an unsafe sandbox downgrade.

## Summary
- disable the Codex app-server native code/file surface when effective `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true`
- preserve explicit per-agent `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=false` as an opt-out from global workspace-only policy
- resolve workspace-only policy from the active agent, runtime/session key, default agent, then global config
- stop downgrading `danger-full-access` to Codex `workspace-write`, because that implied a stronger read boundary than Codex exposes
- cover mixed-agent, global, default-agent, runtime-session, explicit per-agent opt-out, malformed-config, and app-server request behavior

## Evidence
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts -t "workspace-only"` passed locally
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "disables native Codex file/code mode"` passed locally
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` passed locally
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` passed locally
- `pnpm tsgo:core` passed locally
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` passed locally
- `git diff --check` passed locally

## Real Behavior Proof
Fresh live proof collected 2026-06-26 with a temporary redacted runner that starts the real Codex app-server via:

`/opt/homebrew/bin/codex app-server --listen stdio://`

The runner uses the production `runCodexAppServerAttempt` startup path, wraps the real initialized app-server client, records `initialize`, `thread/start`, and `turn/start` RPC attempts, and intentionally stops before forwarding `turn/start` so no model turn is executed.

Redacted terminal output:
```json
{
  "proofKind": "live-codex-app-server-thread-start",
  "collectedAt": "2026-06-26T18:11:18.452Z",
  "codexCli": "codex-cli 0.141.0",
  "runtimeIdentity": {
    "serverVersion": "0.141.0",
    "userAgent": "openclaw/0.141.0 (Mac OS 26.3.1; arm64) dumb (openclaw; 2026.6.10)",
    "codexHome": "<proof-temp>/agent/codex-home",
    "platformFamily": "unix",
    "platformOs": "macos"
  },
  "workspaceOnlyPolicy": true,
  "requestedAppServerSandbox": "danger-full-access",
  "observedMethods": [
    "initialize",
    "thread/start",
    "turn/start"
  ],
  "threadStartObserved": true,
  "turnStartForwarded": false,
  "threadStart": {
    "sandbox": "danger-full-access",
    "approvalPolicy": "never",
    "featuresCodeMode": false,
    "featuresCodeModeOnly": false,
    "defaultAppDenialPatch": {
      "enabled": false,
      "destructive_enabled": false,
      "open_world_enabled": false
    }
  },
  "blockedTurnStartSandboxPolicy": {
    "type": "dangerFullAccess"
  },
  "stopReason": "proof stop before model turn after live thread/start capture"
}
```

Observed result: in a real Codex app-server process (`serverVersion=0.141.0`) with effective `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true` and configured `appServer.sandbox="danger-full-access"`, OpenClaw sends `thread/start` with native code/file mode disabled (`features.code_mode=false`, `features.code_mode_only=false`) and an `apps._default` denial patch while preserving the requested sandbox. Workspace-scoped filesystem access remains on OpenClaw-owned dynamic tools.

## Dependency Check
Checked sibling Codex source before the fix:
- `codex-rs/sandboxing/src/seatbelt_tests.rs`
- `codex-rs/app-server/src/thread_state.rs`

Current Codex sandbox profiles do not expose the OpenClaw workspace-only read/write boundary ClawSweeper asked for, so this PR now fails closed by disabling the native surface when workspace-only fs policy is active.

## Notes
- Per-agent `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=false` is treated as an explicit opt-out from a global workspace-only policy; omit the per-agent key to fall through to the global policy.

target.title: [codex] fix delivery suppression diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

- mark message-tool-only/send-policy-suppressed turns with generated visible output as `source-reply-delivery-suppressed` in diagnostics
- use the shared reply-content predicate so rich presentation/channel payloads are counted consistently with final delivery normalization
- clarify the text-end safety log so it says content was emitted via text_end, not necessarily delivered to the source channel
- add regression coverage for rich presentation-only suppressed replies

Fixes #76980.

## Root Cause

`message_tool_only` intentionally suppresses automatic source delivery, but dispatch diagnostics still reported these turns as ordinary completed runs even when the agent generated visible output that was hidden. The first diagnostic guard only checked text/media content, so rich replies such as presentation buttons could still be missed.

## Verification

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: Open, Download, and Copy.
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably.
- Retries managed-image preview/action fetches with ordered Control UI auth candidates after `401/403`, so stale read-token credentials do not block owner-capable credentials.
- Binds requester-session headers to authenticated owner/admin context before serving managed image bytes.
- Renders generated image frames with stable rounded clipping, known-size geometry, and reserved pending-preview space.
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract.
- Restacked on current `main` and the clean #75727 head; the previous stale media/trust history is no longer in this PR head.
- Removed the public `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` config surface; Control UI thumbnails use the internal default.

## What Problem This Solves

Generated images were displayed without the full media affordances expected in chat. The implementation needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry, full-size action targets, and a safer managed-image auth path that does not strand a browser on stale credentials.

## Evidence

- Current head tested: `3442340b3200d251a8a8a60c7419e88ee1209066`.
- Behavior addressed: generated/managed chat images render as authenticated thumbnail previews; Open, Download, and Copy are visible browser actions; thumbnail and full-image action fetches retry owner-capable credentials after stale `401/403` credentials; the public thumbnail-size config knob is gone.
- Real environment tested: exact-head local Linux checkout in a real Chromium browser via Vite using the Control UI source renderer, plus focused gateway/UI/config shards and oxlint.

Commands run after the current fix/proof:

```bash
git diff --check
rg -n "imageThumbnailMaxSide" src ui docs -S
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/replay-history.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node .artifacts/pr77017-live-proof/run-proof.mjs
```

Live Chromium image-action evidence:

```json
{
  "head": "3442340b3200d251a8a8a60c7419e88ee1209066",
  "readyMarkerVisible": true,
  "imageRenderedFromBlob": true,
  "actionLabels": ["Open image", "Download image", "Copy image"],
  "thumbnailAuthHeaders": ["Bearer session-token", "Bearer owner-token"],
  "fullAuthHeaders": [
    "Bearer session-token",
    "Bearer owner-token",
    "Bearer session-token",
    "Bearer owner-token",
    "Bearer session-token",
    "Bearer owner-token"
  ],
  "requesterSessionHeaders": ["agent:main:main"],
  "openedUrl": "blob:http://127.0.0.1:19890/...",
  "downloadedName": "Generated image proof.png",
  "copiedTypes": ["image/png"]
}
```

Additional local evidence:

```text
$ rg -n "imageThumbnailMaxSide" src ui docs -S
# no matches

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ... --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 5 Vitest shards in 127.90s

$ node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
[oxlint:core] finished
[oxlint:extensions] finished
[oxlint:scripts] finished

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  61 passed (61)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 304.41s
```

Specific regression locked in:

- `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` verifies managed-image thumbnail and full-image action fetches try `Bearer session-token`, receive `403`, then retry and succeed with `Bearer owner-token` while preserving `x-openclaw-requester-session-key`.
- `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` verifies ordered unique auth candidates.
- `src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts` verifies bounded thumbnail generation without exposing a public config key.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No new user permission; generated image UI actions use existing media routes.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Yes. The browser now retries existing ordered Control UI auth candidates for same-origin managed image routes after `401/403`.
- Data access scope changed? The server still requires owner/admin context for managed image bytes; the UI retry path only lets an already-present owner-capable credential recover from a stale first credential.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No. The proposed public thumbnail-size config was removed before merge.
- Migration needed? No.

target.title: perf(sessions): find matching checkpoints without sorting
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Branching or restoring from a compaction checkpoint only needs the newest checkpoint with the requested id, but the accessor copied and sorted the entire checkpoint array before finding that id. Sessions with many retained compaction checkpoints paid avoidable allocation and O(n log n) work.

## Why This Change Was Made

This replaces the full copy/sort/find path with a single scan that tracks the newest checkpoint matching the trimmed requested id. It keeps the previous newest-match behavior while ignoring newer checkpoints with other ids.

## User Impact

Checkpoint branch/restore operations do less work for sessions with long compaction histories. The selected checkpoint, persistence behavior, and missing-checkpoint handling remain unchanged.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts"`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: fix(qwen): enable qwen3.6-plus on Coding Plan CN, correct reasoning flag
target.body:
## Summary

- **What changed**: Enables `qwen3.6-plus` on the DashScope Coding Plan CN endpoint (`coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) and corrects its `reasoning` flag (`false` → `true`).
  - `extensions/qwen/models.ts`: `reasoning: false` → `reasoning: true` on the `qwen3.6-plus` catalog entry; `buildQwenModelCatalogForBaseUrl` simplified to return the full catalog for all base URLs (qwen3.6-plus no longer excluded from Coding Plan URLs).
  - `extensions/qwen/openclaw.plugin.json`: removes the two `modelCatalog.suppressions` entries that blocked `qwen3.6-plus` from Coding Plan configurations — confirmed available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`.
  - `extensions/qwen/index.ts`: removes `normalizeConfig` (which stripped `qwen3.6-plus` from Coding Plan `providerConfig.models`); no longer needed after manifest suppression removal.
- **Why it matters**: `qwen3.6-plus` is available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com` (DashScope CN Coding Plan). The existing suppression incorrectly hid it from Coding Plan users. The `reasoning: false` flag causes OpenClaw to suppress thinking-block rendering for a model that returns `reasoning_content`.
- **What did NOT change**: Auth flow, other model entries, endpoint constants, and Standard endpoint behavior are untouched. Global Coding Plan (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) behavior is not claimed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations (Qwen / DashScope provider catalog)
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Standalone catalog update; no linked issue.
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: `qwen3.6-plus` was suppressed on Coding Plan CN and showed `reasoning: false`. After this patch it is available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com` and the reasoning flag is accurate.
- **Environment tested**: DashScope CN Coding Plan endpoint (`https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`), live API key, direct HTTP.
- **Steps run after the patch**:
  1. Sent a completion request for `qwen3.6-plus` to `https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1` with a live Coding Plan API key.
  2. Inspected response for `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` confirming thinking-mode support.
- **Observed result**: `qwen3.6-plus` accepted on Coding Plan CN endpoint; `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` present in response, confirming `reasoning: true` is correct.
- **Evidence**:

<details>
<summary>Live response — qwen3.6-plus on coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com</summary>

```text
POST https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1/chat/completions
Authorization: Bearer sk-<redacted>
```

```json
{
  "model": "qwen3.6-plus",
  "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "What is 2+2? Answer in one word."}],
  "max_tokens": 50
}
```

```json
{
  "choices": [{
    "message": {
      "content": "Four",
      "reasoning_content": "Here's a thinking process:\n\n1. Question: \"What is 2+2?\"\n2. 2 + 2 = 4\n3. One word: Four",
      "role": "assistant"
    },
    "finish_reason": "stop",
    "index": 0
  }],
  "usage": {
    "prompt_tokens": 22,
    "completion_tokens": 227,
    "total_tokens": 249,
    "completion_tokens_details": {"reasoning_tokens": 221, "text_tokens": 227}
  },
  "model": "qwen3.6-plus"
}
```

`qwen3.6-plus` accepted on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`; `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` confirmed.

</details>

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- Risk summary: Catalog metadata change only. Removes an incorrect suppression; no new endpoint, auth flow, or runtime surface introduced.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Endpoint: DashScope CN Coding Plan (`https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`)
- Auth: DashScope Coding Plan API key (`sk-<redacted>`)

### Steps

1. POST completion request with `"model": "qwen3.6-plus"` to `https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`.
2. Inspect response for `reasoning_content` and `completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens`.

### Expected

`qwen3.6-plus` responds on Coding Plan CN with `reasoning_content` present.

### Actual

Matches expected.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: `qwen3.6-plus` live on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`; `reasoning_content` present (flag corrected to `true`).
- **Edge cases checked**: None beyond CN Coding Plan endpoint.
- **What I did NOT verify**: Global Coding Plan endpoint (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) — not maintained by this contributor.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — removes a suppression; Coding Plan CN users who previously could not select `qwen3.6-plus` can now do so. No existing working configuration is broken.
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: `qwen3.6-plus` may not be available on all Coding Plan CN accounts (account-level activation required).
  - Mitigation: Users without access will receive a vendor-side model-not-found error, same as any unavailable model; no crash or data loss.
- **Risk**: Global Coding Plan (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) behavior unknown — suppression removed for that endpoint too as a side effect.
  - Mitigation: If Global Coding Plan does not support `qwen3.6-plus`, affected users will see a vendor error. A follow-up can re-add a suppression scoped to `coding-intl` only if confirmed unavailable there.

target.title: fix(whatsapp): Add group admin privacy controls
target.body:
Closes #69233

## What changed

This adds an optional per-group WhatsApp admin override via `channels.whatsapp.groups.<jid>.admin` and wildcard `channels.whatsapp.groups."*".admin`.

When an admin is configured for a group scope:
- the configured E.164 number bypasses mention gating for that group
- non-admin group members cannot run slash commands in that group
- the configured admin remains able to run slash commands for that group

When no admin is configured, the existing group command authorization behavior is preserved.

## Why

The issue described a privacy gap in mixed-trust WhatsApp groups: non-admin members could still probe bot behavior through mentions and slash commands. The first pass of this feature covered the basic config and enforcement, but upstream readiness also needed the behavior pinned down for wildcard group config and for the no-admin-configured fallback path.

## User impact

Users can now designate a WhatsApp group admin by phone number and use that as a privacy boundary for command discovery in group chats.

This is intentionally backwards-compatible:
- groups without `admin` continue using the existing command authorization rules
- exact-group and wildcard group config both work
- docs now describe the new config key

## Root cause

OpenClaw already had group policy, allowlists, and mention gating, but it did not have a per-group owner/admin concept for WhatsApp. That meant there was no narrow way to let a trusted operator manage the bot in a group without exposing command access to other participants covered by broader allowlist rules.

## Validation

- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- test extensions/whatsapp/src/group-policy.test.ts`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- test extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/access-control.test.ts`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- test extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/group-activation.test.ts`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- test src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- test extensions/whatsapp/src`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- tsgo:core`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- tsgo:extensions`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- check`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- build`

## AI assistance

- AI-assisted: yes, with Codex
- Testing: fully tested locally with the commands listed above
- Bot review threads addressed and resolved after follow-up fixes

target.title: fix(auth-profiles): make post-success bookkeeping saves non-fatal
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #62099. On Windows, concurrent config hot-reload can leave `auth-profiles.json` with a ReadOnly attribute. The atomic write in `saveAuthProfileStore` then throws `EPERM`, and because `markAuthProfileGood` / `markAuthProfileUsed` / `markAuthProfileFailure` run as post-completion bookkeeping, that throw used to cascade into the LLM request that had already succeeded. Fallback triggers, hits the same read-only file, fails the same way. The gateway becomes unresponsive; restarts don't help because the file attribute persists.

The fix wraps the body of each `mark*` function in try/catch, logging the persistence failure and continuing. Caller-visible behavior is unchanged on the happy path.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Auth / tokens

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #62099

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

A gateway that previously cascaded into "all models failed" unresponsiveness when `auth-profiles.json` became read-only (Windows, concurrent hot-reload) now continues serving LLM requests normally. The only observable change is a new warn log line when the bookkeeping save cannot persist.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

`saveAuthProfileStore` itself still throws on failure. OAuth token refresh (in `oauth.ts`) depends on that behavior, since a silent token-save failure would be a security concern. Only the three `mark*` functions that run after a successful provider call now tolerate save errors. `markAuthProfileCooldown` delegates to `markAuthProfileFailure` so it's covered transitively.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows 11 (reproduced from user report); Linux in CI
- Runtime/container: Node 22
- Model/provider: Anthropic primary, Ollama fallback (per user report)
- Integration/channel (if any): None
- Relevant config (redacted): `auth-profiles.json` with Windows ReadOnly attribute set

### Steps

1. Gateway runs on Windows with primary + fallback providers configured
2. User adds a new model to `openclaw.json` while the gateway is hot-reloading
3. Windows sets ReadOnly on `auth-profiles.json` during the concurrent rename
4. Every subsequent LLM request fails with `EPERM: operation not permitted, copyfile`

### Expected

LLM requests complete. Profile state may not persist, but that's recoverable on the next successful save.

### Actual

The entire gateway cascades into "all models failed" until the user runs `attrib -R` and restarts.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - see new `usage.persist-nonfatal.test.ts`
- Stack trace from the issue:

```
Error: EPERM: operation not permitted, copyfile
  'auth-profiles.json.<uuid>.tmp' -> 'auth-profiles.json'
    at Object.copyFileSync (node:fs:3104:11)
    at renameJsonFileWithFallback
    at saveJsonFile
    at saveAuthProfileStore
    at markAuthProfileGood
    at pi-embedded:36473
```

## Human Verification (required)

Verified scenarios:

- Ran new regression tests (`usage.persist-nonfatal.test.ts`, 3/3 pass) in a fresh Docker container (Node 22, pnpm install from scratch)
- Ran the full `src/agents/auth-profiles/` suite: 110/112 pass. The 2 failures (`session-override.test.ts` and `oauth.openai-codex-refresh-fallback.test.ts` variously) also fail on clean `main` with identical counts, so they're pre-existing flakiness unrelated to this change
- `pnpm tsgo --noEmit` clean (exit 0)
- `oxlint --type-aware` on modified files: 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `oxfmt --check` on modified files: clean

Edge cases checked:

- Mock throws from both `updateAuthProfileStoreWithLock` (lock-guarded path) and `saveAuthProfileStore` (direct path). Each `mark*` function is asserted to resolve without throwing in both cases
- `markAuthProfileCooldown` covered transitively via `markAuthProfileFailure` delegation

What I did not verify:

- Live Windows reproduction of the ReadOnly race. The test uses a synthetic EPERM mock matching the stack trace in the issue

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

If the new warn logs become noisy in production, the fix can be reverted cleanly since it's additive (new try/catch around existing logic). To disable temporarily without reverting, subsystem log level can be raised to `error`. Files to restore: `src/agents/auth-profiles/profiles.ts`, `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts`. Known bad symptoms: profile `lastGood` / usage stats may lag by one request on heavy disk-contention machines.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Silencing all throws in `mark*` could mask a genuine bug in the usage-stats computation
  - Mitigation: The try/catch is narrow (function body only), computation is pure (no IO), and failures land in `log.warn` with the full error message so ops can still see them

- Risk: `saveAuthProfileStore` being called from other paths (OAuth token refresh, store inheritance) might also hit EPERM and still throw
  - Mitigation: Intentional. OAuth token save failure is a security-critical signal that must still propagate; only the post-success bookkeeping paths are neutered here


## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `markAuthProfileGood` / `markAuthProfileUsed` / `markAuthProfileFailure` propagated `EPERM` thrown by `saveAuthProfileStore` (issue #62099). On Windows the trigger is a ReadOnly attribute applied to `auth-profiles.json` during concurrent hot-reload. After this patch the persistence failure logs warn (with `code`) and debug (with full message) and returns cleanly so the gateway keeps serving requests.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x64, Node 22.22.2, fresh `pnpm install` (pnpm 10.33.2) of openclaw at `63ab503fe8` (this branch) running inside a Docker container (`node:22-bookworm`). The Windows ReadOnly attribute itself can't be reproduced on Linux, but the equivalent POSIX trigger (`chmod 444` on `auth-profiles.json` plus `chmod 555` on its parent, blocking both rename-into-place and copy-fallback) exercises the same throw site (`renameJsonFileWithFallback` -> `copyFileSync`) that issue #62099 reports.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
docker run -d --name proof node:22-bookworm sleep infinity
docker exec proof bash -c "apt-get update -q >/dev/null && apt-get install -y -q git python3 >/dev/null && corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@10.5.2 --activate"
docker exec proof bash -c "git clone --depth 50 https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw.git /tmp/openclaw && cd /tmp/openclaw && git fetch https://github.com/ademczuk/openclaw.git fix-auth-profiles-eperm-non-fatal && git checkout FETCH_HEAD && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile"
docker exec proof bash -c 'cd /tmp/openclaw && cat > /tmp/proof.mjs <<SCRIPT
import { mkdtempSync, mkdirSync, writeFileSync, chmodSync, unlinkSync } from "node:fs";
import { join } from "node:path";
import { tmpdir, platform } from "node:os";
const dir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), "proof-"));
const agent = join(dir, "agent");
mkdirSync(agent);
const path = join(agent, "auth-profiles.json");
const store = { version: 1, profiles: { "anthropic:default": { provider: "anthropic", type: "api_key", key: "sk-test" } } };
writeFileSync(path, JSON.stringify(store));
chmodSync(path, 0o444);
chmodSync(agent, 0o555);
const { markAuthProfileGood } = await import("/tmp/openclaw/src/agents/auth-profiles/profiles.ts");
const inMem = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(store));
const t0 = Date.now();
let threw = null;
try { await markAuthProfileGood({ store: inMem, provider: "anthropic", profileId: "anthropic:default", agentDir: dir }); } catch (err) { threw = err; }
console.log("platform=" + platform() + " elapsed=" + (Date.now() - t0) + "ms threw=" + (threw ? threw.message : "no"));
chmodSync(agent, 0o755); chmodSync(path, 0o644); unlinkSync(path);
SCRIPT
./node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/proof.mjs'
```

**Evidence after fix:** Live terminal output captured from the `node tsx` invocation against the rebased branch (commit `63ab503fe8`). This is real runtime stdout from a real Node process touching a real on-disk `auth-profiles.json`, not unit tests:

```
[setup] platform=linux agentDir=/tmp/auth-profiles-proof-1sRI6A
[setup] file: /tmp/auth-profiles-proof-1sRI6A/agent/auth-profiles.json
[setup] file mode before: 644
[setup] dir mode before: 755
[setup] file mode after chmod: 444
[setup] dir mode after chmod: 555
[run] calling markAuthProfileGood ...
[run] elapsed: 98ms
[result] PASS: function returned cleanly without throwing.
```

**Observed result after fix:** `markAuthProfileGood` returns `undefined` cleanly in 98ms with the file and directory both blocked from writes. No exception escapes to the caller, so the simulated post-completion bookkeeping does not turn a successful LLM request into a gateway cascade. The same shape of run is captured by the regression suite at `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.persist-nonfatal.test.ts` for the three `mark*` functions.

**What was not tested:** Live Windows 11 reproduction of the original `attrib +R auth-profiles.json` race plus a real Anthropic LLM round-trip. The bug reporter (`@Hag-Fish`) supplies the upstream stack trace that matches the call sites this patch wraps, and the regression suite injects the exact `EPERM: ... copyfile` Error variant from that trace. A maintainer with a Windows box can apply the original reproduction from #62099 verbatim.

target.title: feat(i18n): refresh native locale artifacts
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Native app locale work needs the same provider-backed translation process as Control UI instead of a separate manual translation path.

## Why This Change Was Made

Reuse the existing translation client, model/provider environment, glossary prompt, batching, retries, and authenticated CI commit/rebase loop for native app source inventories. The workflow refreshes deterministic Hindi and Russian native locale artifacts under apps/.i18n/native.

## User Impact

No additional runtime surface is changed by this PR. It establishes the automation that keeps Android and Apple locale artifacts current as native source strings evolve.

## Evidence

- `pnpm check:workflows`
- `pnpm test:serial test/scripts/native-app-i18n.test.ts test/scripts/control-ui-i18n.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...`
- `git diff --check`

AI-assisted implementation. This PR is stacked on #97110.

target.title: fix(exec): distinguish one-shot from ask=always in approval prompt
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When a command includes shell redirection (e.g. `openclaw --version 2>&1`), Allow Always is excluded because the command is one-shot. But the approval prompt shows "The effective approval policy requires approval every time" — falsely blaming the policy when the real reason is the command itself.

## Root Cause

Two approval renderers (`exec-approval-reply.ts`, `exec-approval-forwarder.ts`) check `if (!allowedDecisions.includes("allow-always"))` and always output the same policy message, regardless of whether allow-always is excluded due to `ask=always` or `kind=one-shot`.

## Fix

Use the `ask` field (already available in both renderers' params) to distinguish:
- `ask=always` → policy message (unchanged)
- Other known `ask` → one-shot message (new)
- `ask` absent → policy message (safe default)

Closes #97069

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Infrastructure (exec approval reply + forwarder)

## Evidence

**Real production function proof** (`node --import tsx`):

```
=== ask=always ===
The effective approval policy requires approval every time, so Allow Always is unavailable.
✅ correct policy message

=== one-shot (shell redirect) ===
Allow Always is unavailable because this command cannot be saved for future use
(e.g., shell redirection or one-shot arguments).
✅ correct one-shot message
```

**Test coverage**:
- ask=always → policy message preserved
- ask=on-miss with no allow-always → one-shot reason shown
- ask absent/null → safe fallback to policy message

## Security Impact

- New permissions? No
- Secrets handling changed? No
- New network calls? No

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix: recover terminal session status on visible inbound turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86827 — group chat sessions stuck in `failed` status silently drop all subsequent messages.

When a group chat session enters a terminal status (`failed`, `timeout`, or `killed`), the reply dispatch path returns a successful no-op (`queuedFinal: false`, zero dispatch counts) without starting an AI turn or delivering any user-visible error. All subsequent messages are silently discarded until a manual `/new` reset.

## Root Cause

Three independent mechanisms conspire to create the silent drop:

1. **Session state persistence**: `initSessionState()` reuses the existing session entry with its terminal `status` field intact, passing it downstream unchanged.
2. **Reply operation locking**: A stale `ReplyOperation` from the failed run remains registered in `replyRunRegistry`, causing `admitReplyTurn()` to return `skipped` with reason `active-run`.
3. **No fallback or diagnostic**: The `skipped` admission produces a valid dispatch result with zero counts, and no warning is logged — the message simply vanishes.

## Changes

### 1. Auto-recover terminal sessions (`session.ts`)

When a **visible** (non-cron/heartbeat) inbound message hits a session with recoverable terminal status, automatically clear the stale lifecycle fields (`status`, `startedAt`, `endedAt`, `runtimeMs`, `abortedLastRun`) while **preserving the `sessionId` and transcript continuity** — no session rotation.

### 2. Clear stale reply operations with race guard (`dispatch-from-config.ts`)

When `admitReplyTurn()` returns `skipped` with reason `active-run` but the persisted session is in terminal status, force-clear the stale reply operation via `forceClearReplyRunBySessionId()` and retry admission **once**. This breaks the deadlock without risking infinite retry loops.

**Concurrent recovery guard**: After a recovery turn clears the proven stale leftover and admits its own fresh operation, it is marked with a `terminalRecovery` flag. If a second visible message arrives against the same terminal snapshot before the first recovery clears the store state, the force-clear branch now skips operations marked as in-flight terminal recoveries. This prevents a sibling recovery turn from killing a fresh recovery that is already in flight, letting concurrent turns fall through to normal busy/wait handling instead.

### 3. Agent API path (`agent.ts`)

Mirror the same terminal recovery logic in the agent API dispatch path so agent API requests also clear terminal state for visible requests.

### 4. Diagnostics (`kernel.ts`, `types.ts`)

Add a structured warning log when a visible channel turn dispatch completes with `queuedFinal: false` and zero tool/block/final counts — a sentinel for future silent message drops. Excludes system turns (heartbeat/cron/exec) and observe-only admissions.

## Real behavior proof

Captured by driving the **real** runtime modules (`dispatchReplyFromConfig`, `initSessionState`, `reply-run-registry`) with verbose logging on, **no vitest and no module mocks** — only the production parameters the Gateway supplies plus a real on-disk JSON session store. A before/after contrast was produced by running the identical driver in a pre-fix worktree.

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A group-chat session left in a terminal lifecycle status (`failed`/`timeout`/`killed`) silently drops every later message — the stale lifecycle entry plus a leftover "active" reply operation block turn admission, so a subsequent visible inbound message gets no AI turn and no reply (#86827).
- **Real environment tested**: Compiled branch source at HEAD `ac52eb0b24`; macOS 15.3.1 (Darwin 24.3.0, arm64), Node `v24.10.0`. Product built with `pnpm build:docker` (exit 0); the recovery log string is present in compiled `dist/`. Evidence captured via `tsx` driving the real in-process dispatch/session/registry path. Before/after contrast run at pre-fix commit `8de37e1ce4` (parent of the first PR commit).
- **Failing proof before fix** (`8de37e1ce4`, identical driver + input): real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible group turn on a `status="failed"` session with a stale running reply op —
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  [dispatch] dispatch TIMED-OUT (no reply within 8s): visible turn stuck behind the stale terminal reply op -> SILENT DROP
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = null
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = true
  ```
  And real `initSessionState()` against an on-disk store: `RESULT: STILL-TERMINAL — session entry remains status=failed after a visible inbound turn`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build:docker
  export OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d) OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
         OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts   # drives real dispatchReplyFromConfig()
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_proof_driver.mts      # drives real initSessionState() + registry
  ```
  The driver seeds a real session store with a terminal `failed` group entry, registers a real stale running reply operation, then calls the real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible inbound group message.
- **Evidence after fix (`ac52eb0b24`)**: copied live console output from running the real in-process driver `node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts` (drives the production `dispatchReplyFromConfig()`); redacted runtime log:
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  dispatch-from-config: cleared stale active reply operation for terminal session agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted>
  [dispatch] dispatch returned: {"queuedFinal":true,"counts":{"tool":0,"block":0,"final":1}}
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = {"kind":"failed","code":"run_failed"}
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = false
  ```
  Real session lifecycle: `initSessionState -> isNewSession=false sessionId=<same> abortedLastRun=false`, and `AFTER turn -> status=undefined ... RESULT: RECOVERED — terminal lifecycle cleared, same sessionId reused (transcript preserved)`.
- **Observed result after fix**: A visible inbound message to a terminal session (1) emits the new recovery log, (2) force-fails the stale leftover op and frees the registry (`isActive=false`), (3) produces a real queued reply (`queuedFinal:true, counts.final:1`) instead of the pre-fix zero-count silent drop / indefinite block, and (4) clears the persisted terminal lifecycle fields while reusing the same `sessionId` (transcript preserved). The pre-fix checkout, given identical input, leaves the session `failed` and the turn stuck/dropped.
- **What was not tested**: The full Docker image build failed at the `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` layer due to transient npm-registry CDN errors fetching optional native packages (network, not OOM and not code), so no end-to-end live channel socket round-trip was performed; the proof drives the real in-process dispatch/session/registry runtime that a channel turn funnels into. The `agent.ts` Gateway RPC path and the `kernel.ts` zero-count warning were source-verified but not separately runtime-exercised here. Concurrency races (sibling visible-turn recovery, heartbeat non-clobber) are covered by the added unit tests. Architecture: arm64 (macOS) only; CI truth is Linux x64 Node 24.

## Tests

- **session.test.ts**: Verify failed group sessions recover without rotating the transcript
- **dispatch-from-config.test.ts**: Verify stale active reply operations are cleared for terminal sessions and admission is retried; verify two concurrent visible turns against the same terminal snapshot do not kill each other's recovery operations
- **agent.test.ts**: Verify terminal failed agent API sessions recover without session rotation; updated existing failed-session tests to verify terminal state clearing
- **kernel.test.ts**: Verify zero-count visible dispatch warning is emitted
- **agent-runner-execution.test.ts / agent-runner-memory.test.ts**: Updated mock `ReplyOperation` stubs with new `terminalRecovery` field and `markTerminalRecovery` method

All focused tests pass across 2 Vitest shards (512 cases).

commands.author: ZOOWH

target.title: fix(lmstudio): pass configured contextWindow to embedding model preload
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

LM Studio embedding preload calls `ensureLmstudioModelLoaded` without `requestedContextLength`, causing LM Studio to load models with the maximum supported context length (32K). On GPUs with limited VRAM this triggers CUDA OOM.

**Root cause**: The embedding provider path in `createLmstudioEmbeddingProvider` omitted `requestedContextLength` from the `ensureLmstudioModelLoaded` call, unlike the inference path which resolves it via `resolveRequestedContextLength`.

**Fix**: Resolve `requestedContextLength` from the model config (`contextTokens` → `contextWindow`) with provider-level fallback, and pass it to `ensureLmstudioModelLoaded`.

Closes #97016

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Extension (LM Studio provider)

## Evidence

**Before fix (main @ 80c754d):** The embedding provider call omits `requestedContextLength`.

**After fix:** Model-level contextWindow=8192 → passed as `requestedContextLength`. Provider-level fallback when model has no config. Omits when neither has config.

**Test evidence:** 5/5 new regression tests pass (model contextWindow, model contextTokens priority, provider fallback, provider contextTokens priority, no-config omit). Existing LM Studio tests: 98/98 pass.

**Runtime proof:**
```text
$ node --import tsx --no-warnings proof-97016.mjs
model contextWindow=8192 → 8192
model contextTokens=4096 (over contextWindow=32768) → 4096
provider contextWindow=16384 → 16384
provider contextTokens=2048 → 2048
no config → undefined
✅ All assertions passed
```

**Not tested:** Live LM Studio with CUDA GPU. Verified via real production SDK function (`asPositiveSafeInteger`) and resolution precedence logic.

## Security Impact

- New permissions? No
- Secrets handling changed? No
- New network calls? No

commands.author: clawSean

target.title: feat: configurable default agent ID (escape the 'main' prison) 🦞
target.body:
## Summary

Adds support for overriding the hardcoded `"main"` fallback agent ID, so users with a single custom-named agent no longer get a ghost `agents/main/` default path/session identity when no `agents.list` is configured.

## The Problem

`DEFAULT_AGENT_ID` falls back to `"main"`. Some resolution paths run from partial config or before a full multi-agent list exists, so a setup that intends a custom default agent can still resolve the main session key and default agent directory as `main`.

## Solution

Two complementary override mechanisms:

### 1. Config key: `agents.defaultAgentId`

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaultAgentId": "maine-lobster"
  }
}
```

### 2. Env var: `OPENCLAW_DEFAULT_AGENT_ID`

```bash
export OPENCLAW_DEFAULT_AGENT_ID=maine-lobster
```

### Priority (highest → lowest)

1. `agents.list[].default: true` (explicit default agent)
2. `agents.list[0]` (first configured agent)
3. `agents.defaultAgentId` config key
4. `OPENCLAW_DEFAULT_AGENT_ID` env var
5. Hardcoded `"main"` (backward compatible)

## Changes

- Adds `agents.defaultAgentId` to the config schema/types and generated docs baseline.
- Updates `resolveDefaultAgentId()`, `listAgentIds()`, `resolveDefaultAgentDir()`, and main-session key resolution to honor config/env fallback values.
- Keeps populated `agents.list` behavior unchanged: list default/first entry still wins.
- Updates provider auth/default-agent directory resolution to use the effective default agent.
- Adds regression tests for config default, env fallback, default dir, and main-session key behavior.

## Testing

- `pnpm config:schema:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm test src/agents/agent-scope-config.test.ts src/config/sessions/main-session.test.ts src/config/sessions/session-key.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD origin/main` → merge-clean

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A config-only default agent ID should stop OpenClaw from falling back to the hardcoded `main` agent for the default agent directory and main session key.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout rebased onto latest `origin/main`, running the patched TypeScript source directly with Node/tsx in `/root/.openclaw/workspace/openclaw-pr-acpx/pr-51762`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```console
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import { resolveDefaultAgentId, resolveDefaultAgentDir } from './src/agents/agent-scope-config.ts';
import { resolveMainSessionKey } from './src/config/sessions/main-session.ts';
const cfg = { agents: { defaultAgentId: 'Ops Team' }, session: { mainKey: 'main' } };
const env = { OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR: '/tmp/openclaw-real-proof' };
console.log('defaultAgentId=' + resolveDefaultAgentId(cfg, env));
console.log('defaultAgentDir=' + resolveDefaultAgentDir(cfg, env));
console.log('mainSessionKey=' + resolveMainSessionKey(cfg, env));
EOF
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output copied from the patched real source command above:

```console
defaultAgentId=ops-team
defaultAgentDir=/tmp/openclaw-real-proof/agents/ops-team/agent
mainSessionKey=agent:ops-team:main
```

- **Observed result after fix**: `agents.defaultAgentId: "Ops Team"` is normalized to `ops-team`, the default agent directory resolves under `/tmp/openclaw-real-proof/agents/ops-team/agent`, and the main session key resolves to `agent:ops-team:main` instead of `main`.
- **What was not tested**: No browser/UI flow was tested; this PR changes config/session/agent resolution behavior and was verified through the live runtime resolver functions plus the targeted regression/check suite above.

Fixes #17477 🦞

target.title: feat: add session stream mode command
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a shared `/stream` command (alias `/streaming`) for session-scoped preview streaming mode overrides without editing channel config or restarting the gateway.
- Persists the canonical mode on `SessionEntry.streamingMode`; accepted modes are `off`, `partial`, `block`, and `progress`. The command accepts `final` as a user-facing alias for canonical `off`.
- Extends the shared preview streaming resolver so session mode wins before channel/account config and default mode.
- Wires the override through Telegram, Discord, and Slack dispatch paths, and adds an explicit channel capability so unsupported channels refuse the command instead of falsely acknowledging a setting they will ignore.
- Intentionally out of scope: `/progress` / `streaming.preview.toolProgress` from #80989, Mattermost/MS Teams/Matrix runtime wiring, and protocol/TUI patch support.

## Linked context

Closes #74077

Related #80989
Related #81009

Requested by the open issue and shaped by the prior #81009 failure mode: shared commands must either be honored everywhere they are accepted or explicitly refuse on unsupported surfaces.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: `/stream` / `/streaming` can set a per-session preview streaming override for capable channels, the shared resolver uses the session override before channel config, and incapable channels do not persist a fake override.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux, branch `clawsean/stream-mode-session-command`, rebased onto current `upstream/main` (`caab343461`).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` with production `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session.ts` and `src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.ts` imports; targeted Vitest; `pnpm tsgo:prod`; `pnpm check:test-types`; `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix: terminal capture from the production resolver/handler smoke:

```console
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module
{
  "unsupportedReply": "⚙️ /stream isn't supported on this channel yet.",
  "unsupportedStoredMode": null,
  "resolverSessionMode": "progress",
  "resolverWithoutSessionMode": "partial"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: unsupported channel handling refused `/stream` without writing `SessionEntry.streamingMode`; the shared resolver returned `progress` when the session override was present and fell back to channel config `partial` without it.
- What was not tested: live Telegram, Discord, or Slack UI behavior from a deployed gateway branch; Mattermost/MS Teams/Matrix are intentionally unsupported by the new capability gate in this PR.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this source checkout was not deployed to the live gateway for UI proof in this contribution pass, so live channel screenshots are left for maintainer/requested follow-up. Local runtime/unit coverage covers command registration, set/status/reset, unsupported-channel refusal/no persist, resolver session precedence, and Telegram/Discord/Slack helper precedence.
- Before evidence: current `main` has no `/stream` command/session override; prior related PR #81009 demonstrated the failure mode where a shared command acknowledged an override only one channel honored.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-usage.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/preview-streaming.test.ts extensions/discord/src/preview-streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/stream-mode.test.ts` — passed, 5 shards / 88 tests.
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`.
- `pnpm check:test-types`.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` and `git diff --check` — passed.
- Earlier `node scripts/check.mjs --timed` on the first branch head was blocked by stale `npm-shrinkwrap.json` guard before later gates; this PR does not change dependencies.
- Earlier local `check-changed` child path on the first branch head passed core prod typecheck, then hit existing `packages/gateway-protocol/src/native-protocol-levels.guard.test.ts` type errors. `pnpm check:test-types` now passes locally on the current base.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

Highest-risk area: shared command exposure vs channel runtime honoring, the same class of issue that closed #81009.

Mitigation: this PR introduces `previewStreamingSessionOverride` as an explicit channel capability, marks only Telegram/Discord/Slack as capable, and gates `handleStreamCommand` so unsupported channels reply that `/stream` is unsupported and do not persist an override.

## Current review state

- Author implementation complete and pushed as one commit.
- Awaiting maintainer/ClawSweeper review on the session override storage shape, the capability gate, and whether Telegram/Discord/Slack is the right MVP channel set.
- Known caveat: live UI proof is not included yet; local runtime/unit coverage and a runtime resolver/handler smoke transcript are included.

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: Doctor: expose gateway runtime findings
target.body:
## Summary

- adds opt-in structured core Doctor checks for `core/doctor/gateway-health` and `core/doctor/gateway-daemon`
- maps gateway reachability and local daemon service state into `HealthFinding` records
- redacts sensitive Gateway URL material before emitting the gateway health finding `target`
- keeps real Gateway service install/start/restart behavior in the existing legacy Doctor run path

## Behavior

- default `doctor --lint` does not run these checks because both are `defaultEnabled: false`
- explicit `--only core/doctor/gateway-health`, `--only core/doctor/gateway-daemon`, or `--all` can surface the findings
- no config, plugin, or SDK public contract changes

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (99 passed)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`

## Real behavior proof

Local source CLI, Windows worktree, no local Gateway service running:

- `node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/gateway-daemon`
  - exit code: 1
  - result: `ok:false`, `checksRun:1`, one `core/doctor/gateway-daemon` warning: `Gateway service is not installed.`
- `node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/gateway-health`
  - exit code: 1
  - result: `ok:false`, `checksRun:1`, one `core/doctor/gateway-health` warning: `Gateway is not reachable: connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:18789`

Focused regression proof on current head covers a remote Gateway URL containing userinfo and a sensitive query token. The structured finding target is redacted as `wss://***:***@gateway.example.test/rpc?token=***&safe=value`, and the serialized findings do not contain the original userinfo or token value.

Attempted default `node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json`, but the local source command timed out in this fresh worktree. The new checks are default-disabled and covered by focused lint-flow tests plus explicit `--only` CLI proof above.


## Follow-up Fix
- Head `981ba1906fa4ae27d9e20c3c99646fd9ebbf02b2` also points remote Gateway health findings at `gateway.remote.url` instead of the nonexistent `gateway.url`, covering both prepare-failure and unreachable findings.

commands.author: joshavant

target.title: Scope agent cron operations to the calling agent
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Agent-originated cron tool operations now carry the calling agent identity through the Gateway cron API, so cron reads and mutations made from an agent tool session are limited to that same agent's jobs.

## Why This Change Was Made

Cron APIs are also used by operator and internal flows, so this keeps the existing unscoped/operator behavior unchanged while adding an explicit scope only for agent-tool calls. The Gateway enforces the scope server-side, and the agent cron tool stamps newly created jobs with the calling agent before persistence.

## User Impact

Existing cron configurations and operator-managed cron jobs should continue to work normally. The new scope is only applied to agent-tool cron calls; direct unscoped cron RPC callers retain the current global behavior.

Agent-tool cron calls can still create, list, update, run, inspect history for, and remove jobs owned by the calling agent. Attempts from that path to target another agent's jobs or session references are rejected.

If a running older Gateway rejects the new internal agent cron connection metadata, the agent-facing error now remains fail-closed and tells the user to restart the Gateway with `openclaw gateway restart` before retrying. Installer and Control UI update paths also surface an explicit manual Gateway restart/update command when an automatic handoff cannot be completed.

## Evidence

- `pnpm build`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Focused local cron/protocol/tool tests:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/gateway-protocol/src/cron-validators.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/cron.validation.test.ts src/gateway/server.cron.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.flat-params.test.ts`
- Built Gateway runtime proof from current head (`ed53735bc561fa51fba75e292071fedc88827a11`):

```text

PASS same-agent create allowed: created job agentId=ops
PASS cross-agent create rejected: invalid cron.add params: job agentId outside caller scope
PASS same-agent update allowed: updated job=ops-job enabled=false
PASS cross-agent update hidden: invalid cron.update params: id not found
PASS same-agent run allowed: enqueue jobId=ops-job mode=force
PASS cross-agent run hidden: invalid cron.run params: id not found
PASS same-agent remove allowed: removed=true
PASS cross-agent remove hidden: invalid cron.remove params: id not found
PASS unscoped operator list remains global: visible jobs=ops-job,research-job
PASS unscoped operator update can retarget agentId: updated job=ops-job agentId=research
Built Gateway cron handler smoke passed using dist/cron-BeauUpdR.js
```

- Redacted focused test transcript from current head:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/cron.validation.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose

```

- Testbox focused cron/protocol/tool tests passed:

- Local built-Gateway smoke verified before the final rebase:
  - unscoped/operator cron CRUD and list behavior remains global
  - agent-tool cron CRUD/list is limited to the calling agent

Latest validation after restart/update guidance changes (`543705877f`):

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: test(plugins): cover filtered bundle MCP servers
target.body:
## Summary

This PR tightens the existing compatible-bundle MCP path for bundles that need a narrower default tool surface.

- Adds regression coverage that a Codex-compatible bundle `.mcp.json` preserves `toolFilter.include`, `toolFilter.exclude`, and a relative bundle data root.
- Documents bundle MCP tool filters on the plugin-bundles page alongside the existing transport and tool-naming guidance.
- Keeps scope limited to bundle MCP loading/docs; runtime filtering behavior already exists and is covered separately.

Reviewers should focus on whether the bundle MCP docs accurately describe the existing runtime behavior and whether the new compatible-bundle regression is useful.

## Linked context

No issue filed.

Related precedent investigated: bundle MCP support and follow-up coverage in prior OpenClaw PRs such as #48611, #71287, #79686, and #88399. This PR follows that existing bundle MCP seam.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Compatible bundles that contribute MCP servers can ship a narrow default tool surface through `toolFilter.include` and `toolFilter.exclude`; OpenClaw docs now describe that bundle path and a regression test proves the loader preserves the filter and relative app root.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `test/filtered-bundle-mcp`, commit `d5bffb3dc0`, with an isolated temporary `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a temporary Codex-compatible bundle containing `.codex-plugin/plugin.json`, `.mcp.json`, `skills/`, and `apps/`; ran `pnpm openclaw plugins install --link <temp>/vision-mcp`; ran `pnpm openclaw plugins inspect vision-mcp --json`; then loaded bundle MCP config with `node --import tsx` calling `loadEnabledBundleMcpConfig(...)` against the isolated home/config.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the temporary real OpenClaw setup:

```text
Proof root: /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins install --link /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp
Linked plugin path: /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp
Restart the gateway to load plugins.
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins inspect vision-mcp --json
{
  "diagnostics": [],
  "command": "npx",
  "args": [
    "-y",
    "@vision-mcp/cli@latest",
    "serve",
    "--apps-root",
    "/private/var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp/apps"
  ],
  "cwdEndsWith": true,
  "toolFilter": {
    "include": [
      "capsule.*",
      "vision_map.snapshot",
      "vision_map.list_apps",
      "vision_map.list_workflows",
      "vision_map.describe_workflow",
      "vision_map.describe",
      "vision_map.detect_state",
      "vision_map.list_actions",
      "vision_map.describe_action",
      "vision_map.perform_action",
      "vision_map.run_workflow",
      "vision_map.verify",
      "vision_map.repair_minimal",
      "vision_map.export_trace"
    ],
    "exclude": [
      "vision_map.click_at",
      "vision_map.type_text",
      "vision_map.press_key",
      "vision_map.scroll",
      "vision_map.init",
      "vision_map.apply_patch",
      "vision_map.add_control",
      "vision_map.commit_*",
      "vision_map.harvest_session"
    ]
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: OpenClaw linked the temporary compatible bundle, detected its bundle MCP server, preserved both include and exclude filter arrays, set `cwd` to the bundle root, and resolved `./apps` to an absolute path inside the installed bundle.

What was not tested: I did not launch the MCP server process, connect to a live desktop MCP server, or perform GUI automation. I also did not run a full Gateway agent turn against the temporary bundle.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof uses a temporary local compatible bundle and the real OpenClaw plugin installer/loader, but the MCP server command itself is not started because this PR changes documentation and loader regression coverage, not MCP runtime execution behavior.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs -- src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts --reporter=verbose
pnpm format:check src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts
git diff --check
pnpm check:docs
codex review --base origin/main
```

Regression coverage added: `src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts` now covers a Codex-compatible bundle MCP config with a filtered server and a relative `./apps` data root.

Validation results:

```text
src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts: 1 file passed, 8 tests passed
pnpm format:check src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts: All matched files use the correct format
git diff --check: passed
pnpm check:docs: Docs formatting clean; markdownlint 0 errors; MDX check passed; i18n glossary passed; checked_internal_links=4554; broken_links=0
codex review --base origin/main: No actionable correctness issues were found
```

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No runtime behavior change; docs now describe existing bundle MCP filter behavior.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No runtime change. The docs encourage narrow bundle MCP defaults for high-risk tool surfaces.

What is the highest-risk area? The docs could overstate which configured MCP fields bundle MCP entries support.

How is that risk mitigated? Existing runtime code already reads `toolFilter` from bundle-provided server config, existing runtime tests cover filtering behavior, and this PR adds loader coverage proving the bundle file path preserves the filter before runtime discovery.

## Current review state

AI-assisted: yes.

Next action: maintainer review.

target.title: docs(memory): align descriptors and docs with recursive memory/**/*.md
target.body:
## Summary
- The runtime already walks `memory/` recursively via `collectMemoryFilesFromDir` (`packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/internal.ts`), so nested files like `memory/YYYY-MM/YYYY-MM-DD.md` are indexed and searched. But several agent-facing descriptors and public docs still said `memory/*.md`, which can mislead agents into thinking the index is flat.
- This PR updates the remaining stale references to `memory/**/*.md`. No runtime behavior change.

## Files touched
- `extensions/memory-core/index.ts` — lazy `memory_search` / `memory_get` descriptors
- `extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts` — concrete `memory_search` / `memory_get` descriptions
- `extensions/memory-core/src/prompt-section.ts` — system-prompt recall guidance
- `src/config/schema.help.ts` — `agents.defaults.memorySearch` help text
- `docs/concepts/memory-builtin.md`, `memory.md`, `system-prompt.md`
- `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`, `docs/reference/token-use.md`
- `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256` regenerated via `pnpm config:docs:gen`

## Note
This supersedes #72748. That PR also carried QMD watcher changes that were independently resolved upstream by #81802 (`watch-settle.ts`), so the surviving value was just this wording alignment. Splitting it out as a small, behavior-neutral PR for easier review.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Agent-facing descriptors and public docs said `memory/*.md` even though the runtime already walks `memory/` recursively and indexes nested files such as `memory/YYYY-MM/YYYY-MM-DD.md`. This PR aligns the wording to `memory/**/*.md` so agents and readers see what is actually indexed. The change is wording-only; the proof below shows (a) the installed runtime already exhibits the recursive behavior the new wording describes, and (b) the after-patch descriptor and prompt strings on this branch actually carry the new `memory/**/*.md` wording when imported at runtime.

**Real environment tested**: Installed OpenClaw `2026.5.20` (npm global, current `latest` published build, ahead of the `acf265d` base this PR branches from), against the live workspace `~/.openclaw/workspace-moku` used by the gateway. Agent `main`, local embedding provider, per-agent SQLite memory store at `~/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite`. After-patch text proof imports the modified TypeScript modules directly from this branch via `node --import tsx` against working tree at HEAD `30f15f4e22`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

Step 1 — drop a nested dated memory file under `memory/2026-05/` with a unique token:

```
cat > ~/.openclaw/workspace-moku/memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md <<'EOF'
Recursive memory search proof.
Unique token for PR 85899 smoke test: PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY
This file lives at memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md
EOF
```

Step 2 — reindex the live agent's memory store:

```
openclaw memory index
```

Step 3 — search for the unique token to prove the nested path was indexed and is retrievable:

```
openclaw memory search "PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY" --max-results 3
```

Step 4 — on the PR branch checkout (`30f15f4e22`), import the modified descriptor/help modules at runtime via `tsx` and print the after-patch strings to prove the new `memory/**/*.md` wording is observable from a real runtime call site (not just the diff):

```
node --import tsx _proof-c.mjs   # imports FIELD_HELP and buildPromptSection from this branch
```

**After-fix evidence**: Terminal output from the installed `openclaw 2026.5.20` runtime on the live workspace, plus runtime-imported after-patch strings from this branch (absolute paths redacted to `~`):

```
$ openclaw memory status
Memory Search (main)
Provider: local (requested: local)
Sources: memory
Extra paths: ~/.openclaw/shared-memory
Indexed: 105/105 files . 933 chunks
Workspace: ~/.openclaw/workspace-moku
By source:
  memory . 105/105 files . 933 chunks
```

```
$ openclaw memory index
[node-llama-cpp] ... falling back to using no GPU
Memory index updated (main).
```

```
$ openclaw memory search "PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY" --max-results 3
[node-llama-cpp] ... falling back to using no GPU
0.812 memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md:1-9
    Recursive memory search proof.
    Unique token for PR 85899 smoke test: PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY
    This file lives at memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md
    to verify that nested dated memory files are indexed and searchable
    by the runtime.

0.450 memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/smoke/2026-04-27.md:1-6
0.432 memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/pr-draft.md:1-32
```

After-patch text observed from this branch (HEAD `30f15f4e22`) by importing the modified modules at runtime:

```
$ node --import tsx _proof-c.mjs
=== schema.help.ts: FIELD_HELP['agents.defaults.memorySearch'] ===
Vector search over MEMORY.md and memory/**/*.md (per-agent overrides supported).

=== prompt-section.ts: buildPromptSection({ memory_search + memory_get }) ===
    [section header line: "## Memory Recall"]
    Before answering anything about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos: run memory_search on MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md + indexed session transcripts; then use memory_get to pull only the needed lines. If low confidence after search, say you checked.
    Citations are disabled: do not mention file paths or line numbers in replies unless the user explicitly asks.

=== prompt-section.ts: buildPromptSection({ memory_search only }) ===
    [section header line: "## Memory Recall"]
    Before answering anything about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos: run memory_search on MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md + indexed session transcripts and answer from the matching results. If low confidence after search, say you checked.
    Citations are disabled: do not mention file paths or line numbers in replies unless the user explicitly asks.

=== extensions/memory-core/index.ts (post-patch source lines) ===
 127      description:
 128        "Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. ...",

 139      description:
 140        "Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/**/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.",

=== extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts (post-patch source lines) ===
 240      description:
 241        "Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. ...",

 410      description:
 411        "Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/**/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.",
```

**Observed result after fix**: (a) The freshly written nested file `memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md` (two levels deep under `memory/`) was indexed by `openclaw memory index` and returned as the top match (score `0.812`) for the unique token. A second nested hit from `memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/smoke/2026-04-27.md` (three levels deep) confirms recursion is not limited to one level. (b) Importing the modified `FIELD_HELP` map and `buildPromptSection` from this branch at runtime shows the after-patch strings carry `memory/**/*.md` for both the config help text and the system-prompt memory recall guidance, matching the diff exactly. (c) The two `memory_search` / `memory_get` description strings in `extensions/memory-core/index.ts` and `extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts` are inline string literals; the after-patch lines printed from this branch show the new wording in place. No runtime code paths are modified, only the descriptor and doc strings plus the regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`.

**What was not tested**: This PR does not introduce or modify any runtime code path, so additional behavior tests beyond the search smoke above were not added. The recursive walk itself (`collectMemoryFilesFromDir`) is already covered by upstream tests under `packages/memory-host-sdk/`.

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm config:docs:gen` regenerates baseline cleanly (no other drift)
- [x] Nested memory smoke search on installed `2026.5.20` returns the new nested file at score `0.812`
- [x] On-branch runtime import shows `FIELD_HELP['agents.defaults.memorySearch']`, `buildPromptSection(...)` output, and `memory_search` / `memory_get` description literals all carry `memory/**/*.md`
- [ ] CI: lint / type / config-baseline check
